title: helianthemum × sulphureum var. cupreum
intro: Helianthemum × sulphureum var. cupreum, also known as Rockrose, is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the family Cistaceae. Traditionally, Rockrose has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat various skin conditions, wounds, and inflammation, due to its antibacterial and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, Rockrose is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
